nodes.name: coding-style.md
nodes.summary: Angular coding style rule document covering file naming conventions, component structure, dependency injection with `inject()`, signals API (signal/computed/linkedSignal/resource/effect), template control flow, reactive forms, component styles, and change detection — targeting Angular 17+ with standalone components.
nodes.languageNotes: Covers Angular's modern signals API including `linkedSignal` for writable derived state and the `resource` primitive for async data fetching into signals.

nodes.name: hooks.md
nodes.summary: Minimal ECC hook configuration rule for Angular projects, specifying PostToolUse hooks for auto-formatting Angular files after edits and Stop hooks for session persistence.

nodes.name: patterns.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive Angular architecture pattern guide covering smart/dumb component split, service layer design, async data with `resource`, signal state management, subscription cleanup, routing with functional guards and data resolvers, dependency injection patterns, HTTP interceptors, RxJS operators, reactive forms, rendering strategies, and accessibility best practices.
nodes.languageNotes: Emphasizes Angular's modern standalone component model, signal-based state, and functional DI patterns (inject() function, InjectionToken) over class-based approaches.

nodes.name: security.md
nodes.summary: Angular security rule document addressing XSS prevention (DomSanitizer, CSP, avoiding bypassSecurityTrust), HTTP security with CSRF tokens and HTTPS enforcement, secret management, route guard patterns, SSR-specific security concerns, and Content Security Policy configuration.

nodes.name: testing.md
nodes.summary: Angular testing rule document covering test runner setup (Karma/Jest/Vitest), TestBed configuration, signal input testing, component harnesses, router testing, async testing utilities (fakeAsync/tick), HTTP testing with HttpTestingController, service unit tests, E2E testing with Playwright, and coverage thresholds.
nodes.languageNotes: Covers both TestBed-based component tests and lightweight service unit tests, with Angular CDK component harnesses recommended over direct DOM querying.
